python3 << 'PYEOF'
import re
from html.parser import HTMLParser

# Try to extract pricing from 1Password
with open('/tmp/1password_pricing.html', 'r') as f:
    content = f.read()

# Look for price patterns
price_patterns = re.findall(r'\$[\d,]+(?:\.\d+)?(?:\s*/\s*(?:month|mo|user|seat|yr|year))?', content, re.IGNORECASE)
print("1Password price patterns found:")
for p in set(price_patterns):
    print(f"  {p}")

# Also look for tier names
tier_patterns = re.findall(r'"(Business|Teams|Enterprise|Family|Individual)"', content)
print("\nTier names found:")
for t in set(tier_patterns):
    print(f"  {t}")
PYEOF

python3 << 'PYEOF'
import re

# 1Password - look for pricing in JSON data embedded in the page
with open('/tmp/1password_pricing.html', 'r') as f:
    content = f.read()

# Look for JSON-like pricing data
json_blocks = re.findall(r'\{[^{}]*"price"[^{}]*\}', content)
for block in json_blocks[:5]:
    print("1Password JSON pricing block:")
    print(block[:500])
    print("---")

# Also search for specific tier pricing mentions
for tier in ['Business', 'Teams', 'Enterprise']:
    matches = re.findall(f'{tier}[^<>{{}}]*?\\$[\d,]+(?:\.\d+)?[^<>{{}}]*?(?:month|mo|per)', content, re.IGNORECASE)
    if matches:
        print(f"\n{tier} mentions:")
        for m in matches[:3]:
            print(f"  {m[:200]}")
PYEOF

# Try to find pricing in the 1Password page more carefully
python3 << 'PYEOF'
import re

with open('/tmp/1password_pricing.html', 'r') as f:
    content = f.read()

# Look for all dollar amounts with context
matches = re.findall(r'.{0,100}\$[\d,]+(?:\.\d+)?(?:\s*/\s*(?:month|mo|user|seat|yr|year))?.{0,100}', content, re.IGNORECASE)
print(f"Found {len(matches)} price mentions")
for m in matches[:20]:
    # Clean up
    clean = re.sub(r'\s+', ' ', m).strip()
    print(f"  {clean[:200]}")
PYEOF

python3 << 'PYEOF'
import re, json

with open('/tmp/1password_pricing.html', 'r') as f:
    content = f.read()

# Extract all pricing JSON blocks
pricing_blocks = re.findall(r'"type":"plansOverviewPricingDetailsNew"[^}]*\}[^}]*\}', content)
print("=== 1Password Pricing Details ===")
for block in pricing_blocks:
    # Try to extract key fields
    title = re.search(r'"entryTitle":"([^"]+)"', block)
    price_annual = re.search(r'"priceAnnual":"([^"]+)"', block)
    price_monthly = re.search(r'"priceMonthly":"([^"]+)"', block)
    details_annual = re.search(r'"postPriceDetailsAnnual":"([^"]+)"', block)
    details_monthly = re.search(r'"postPriceDetailsMonthly":"([^"]+)"', block)
    
    if title:
        print(f"\n  Plan: {title.group(1)}")
        if price_annual:
            print(f"    Annual: {price_annual.group(1)}")
        if price_monthly:
            print(f"    Monthly: {price_monthly.group(1)}")
        if details_annual:
            print(f"    Annual details: {details_annual.group(1)}")
        if details_monthly:
            print(f"    Monthly details: {details_monthly.group(1)}")
PYEOF

# Get 1Password Enterprise pricing

import sys, re
content = sys.stdin.read()
print(f'1Password Enterprise page: {len(content)} bytes')
# Look for pricing mentions
prices = re.findall(r'.{0,80}\$[\d,]+(?:\.\d+)?(?:\s*/\s*(?:month|mo|user|seat|yr|year))?.{0,80}', content, re.IGNORECASE)
for p in prices[:10]:
    print(f'  {re.sub(r\"\\s+\", \" \", p).strip()[:200]}')
"

# Try to find Bitwarden pricing from their GitHub or other sources
curl -sL "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/bitwarden/server/main/utilities/Server/Server/Program.cs" 2>/dev/null | head -5
# Try their pricing page with a different user agent
curl -sL -H "User-Agent: Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/120.0.0.0 Safari/537.36" "https://bitwarden.com/pricing/" 2>/dev/null | python3 -c "
import sys, re
content = sys.stdin.read()
print(f'Bitwarden pricing page with UA: {len(content)} bytes')
# Look for pricing in script tags
scripts = re.findall(r'<script[^>]*>(.*?)</script>', content, re.DOTALL)
for s in scripts:
    if 'price' in s.lower() or '\$' in s:
        prices = re.findall(r'\$[\d,]+(?:\.\d+)?', s)
        if prices:
            print(f'  Found prices in script: {set(prices)}')
            break
"

# Try to find Bitwarden pricing from their serverless API or pricing endpoint
curl -sL "https://bitwarden.com/pricing/" 2>/dev/null | python3 -c "
import sys, re
content = sys.stdin.read()
# Look for any data attributes or JSON-LD
data_attrs = re.findall(r'data-[a-z]+=\"([^\"]*price[^\"]*)\"', content, re.IGNORECASE)
print(f'Data attributes with price: {data_attrs[:10]}')
# Look for JSON-LD
json_ld = re.findall(r'<script type=\"application/ld\+json\">(.*?)</script>', content, re.DOTALL)
print(f'JSON-LD blocks: {len(json_ld)}')
# Look for any embedded pricing data
pricing_json = re.findall(r'\"(?:price|cost|amount)\"[^\}]*\}', content, re.IGNORECASE)
for p in pricing_json[:5]:
    print(f'  Pricing JSON: {p[:200]}')
"

# Try to find Bitwarden pricing from their server-side rendered content
curl -sL "https://bitwarden.com/pricing/" 2>/dev/null | python3 -c "
import sys, re
content = sys.stdin.read()
# Look for pricing in the Nuxt/Next.js data
# Check for __NUXT__ or __NEXT_DATA__
nuxt_data = re.findall(r'__NUXT__\s*=\s*(.*?);?\s*</script>', content, re.DOTALL)
next_data = re.findall(r'__NEXT_DATA__\s*=\s*(.*?);?\s*</script>', content, re.DOTALL)
print(f'Nuxt data blocks: {len(nuxt_data)}')
print(f'Next data blocks: {len(next_data)}')

# Look for pricing in any script tag
scripts = re.findall(r'<script[^>]*>(.*?)</script>', content, re.DOTALL)
for i, s in enumerate(scripts):
    if 'price' in s.lower() and len(s) > 100:
        prices = re.findall(r'\$[\d,]+(?:\.\d+)?', s)
        if prices:
            print(f'Script {i}: prices = {set(prices)}')
            # Print context
            for p in set(prices):
                idx = s.find(p)
                if idx >= 0:
                    print(f'  Context: ...{s[max(0,idx-100):idx+len(p)+100]}...')
"

# Try to get pricing from a well-known comparison site with static content
curl -sL "https://www.pcmag.com/picks/the-best-password-managers" 2>/dev/null | python3 -c "
import sys, re
content = sys.stdin.read()
# Look for Bitwarden pricing specifically
bw_section = re.search(r'bitwarden.{0,3000}', content, re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL)
if bw_section:
    text = bw_section.group(0)
    prices = re.findall(r'\$[\d,]+(?:\.\d+)?(?:\s*/\s*(?:month|mo|user|seat|yr|year))?', text, re.IGNORECASE)
    print(f'Bitwarden section prices: {set(prices)}')
    # Also look for any dollar amounts
    all_prices = re.findall(r'\$[\d,]+(?:\.\d+)?', text)
    print(f'All dollar amounts: {set(all_prices)}')
"

python3 << 'PYEOF'
import urllib.request
import re
import json

# Try to find Bitwarden pricing from their open source code
urls = [
    "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/bitwarden/server/main/utilities/Server/Server/Models/Static/PolicyDataModel.cs",
    "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/bitwarden/server/main/utilities/Server/Server/Services/Implementations/NotificationHubPushRegistrationService.cs",
    "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/bitwarden/clients/main/apps/web/src/app/admin-console/organizations/settings/org-settings.component.html",
    "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/bitwarden/clients/main/libs/angular/src/admin-console/organizations/settings/org-settings.component.html",
]

for url in urls:
    try:
        req = urllib.request.Request(url, headers={'User-Agent': 'Mozilla/5.0'})
        with urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=10) as response:
            content = response.read().decode('utf-8')
            prices = re.findall(r'\$[\d,]+(?:\.\d+)?', content)
            if prices:
                print(f'{url}: {set(prices)}')
    except Exception as e:
        print(f'{url}: {e}')
PYEOF

# Try to get Bitwarden pricing from their API
curl -sL "https://vault.bitwarden.com/api/accounts/plans" 2>/dev/null | python3 -c "
import sys, json
try:
    data = json.load(sys.stdin)
    print(f'Plans API response: {len(json.dumps(data))} bytes')
    if isinstance(data, dict) and 'data' in data:
        for plan in data['data'][:5]:
            pm = plan.get('PasswordManager', {})
            print(f'  Plan: {plan.get(\"name\", \"\")} ({plan.get(\"type\", \"\")})')
            print(f'    Base price: {pm.get(\"basePrice\", \"\")}')
            print(f'    Seat price: {pm.get(\"seatPrice\", \"\")}')
            print(f'    Base seats: {pm.get(\"baseSeats\", \"\")}')
    elif isinstance(data, list):
        for plan in data[:5]:
            pm = plan.get('PasswordManager', {})
            print(f'  Plan: {plan.get(\"name\", \"\")} ({plan.get(\"type\", \"\")})')
            print(f'    Base price: {pm.get(\"basePrice\", \"\")}')
            print(f'    Seat price: {pm.get(\"seatPrice\", \"\")}')
            print(f'    Base seats: {pm.get(\"baseSeats\", \"\")}')
except Exception as e:
    print(f'Error: {e}')
    print(sys.stdin.read()[:500])
"

# Get Dashlane pricing from their page
curl -sL "https://www.dashlane.com/pricing" 2>/dev/null | python3 -c "
import sys, re
content = sys.stdin.read()
# Look for pricing in JSON data
json_blocks = re.findall(r'\{[^{}]*\"price\"[^{}]*\}', content)
for block in json_blocks[:10]:
    print(block[:300])
    print('---')
# Also look for pricing in text
prices = re.findall(r'.{0,100}\$[\d,]+(?:\.\d+)?(?:\s*/\s*(?:month|mo|user|seat|yr|year))?.{0,100}', content, re.IGNORECASE)
for p in prices[:10]:
    clean = re.sub(r'\s+', ' ', p).strip()
    print(f'  {clean[:200]}')
"

python3 << 'PYEOF'
import re

# 1Password - extract all pricing data from the page
with open('/tmp/1password_pricing.html', 'r') as f:
    content = f.read()

# Extract all pricing JSON blocks
pricing_blocks = re.findall(r'"type":"plansOverviewPricingDetailsNew"[^}]*\}[^}]*\}', content)
print("=== 1Password Pricing Details ===")
for block in pricing_blocks:
    # Try to extract key fields
    title = re.search(r'"entryTitle":"([^"]+)"', block)
    price_annual = re.search(r'"priceAnnual":"([^"]+)"', block)
    price_monthly = re.search(r'"priceMonthly":"([^"]+)"', block)
    details_annual = re.search(r'"postPriceDetailsAnnual":"([^"]+)"', block)
    details_monthly = re.search(r'"postPriceDetailsMonthly":"([^"]+)"', block)
    
    if title:
        print(f"\n  Plan: {title.group(1)}")
        if price_annual:
            print(f"    Annual: {price_annual.group(1)}")
        if price_monthly:
            print(f"    Monthly: {price_monthly.group(1)}")
        if details_annual:
            print(f"    Annual details: {details_annual.group(1)}")
        if details_monthly:
            print(f"    Monthly details: {details_monthly.group(1)}")
PYEOF

    clean = re.sub(r'\s+', ' ', p).strip()
    print(f'  {clean[:200]}')
